summary: `install` reads the lock on disk under its path: a root carrying `.temper/lock.toml` skips the settled represent question and converges on the lock; a path that IS a workspace refuses loud

edit.description: Two halves of one job — install resolving its path argument against the locks already on disk. (1) The represent decision (398-413): `yes` ⇒ Yes, `no_represent` ⇒ No, else `ask_represent()` (620-633, which defaults an unattended terminal to No). A root whose `.temper/lock.toml` exists has answered the fork on disk, so neither the prompt nor its conservative default applies: detect it and take the represented path, placements following the lock's current contents. `--no-represent` against such a root asserts the false half of a settled fork — refuse as a usage error naming the lock, never silently place less than the lock justifies. (2) The install arm resolves `path` as a harness root unconditionally, so a path that IS a workspace (`lock.toml` directly under it) is treated as a raw unrepresented root: it discovers the workspace's own files and would scaffold `<path>/.claude/settings.json` inside the workspace — silent nonsense where invariant 6 demands loud. Refuse, naming the enclosing root as the argument meant. Consolidation, not a second resolver (`specs/process/engineering.md`, 'One job, one home'): `harness_diagnostics` (658-680) already answers exactly this question three ways off the `LOCK_FILE` const c7409f0 added — extend that surface rather than hand-rolling install's own lock probe.

`REPRESENT_QUESTION` and its doc (175-178) carry the one-genuine-fork rule; state there that the question is asked only where the fork is live (no lock on disk). `render_discovery` (330) — the report printed before the question — names the already-represented root, so a skipped question is visible rather than invisible state.

tests.asserts: a represented root (lock present), neither flag, no tty: takes the represented path — the lock's placements, no prompt; `--no-represent` against a represented root refuses loud; a workspace dir passed as the path refuses instead of scaffolding a `.claude/` inside it

acceptance: On this repo, non-interactive `temper install . --dry-run` places the 3 managed-by notes `check .` demands — the probe and the on-ramp converge — asking no question the lock has already answered; `temper install .temper` refuses rather than scaffolding into the workspace.
notes: Both premises re-probed live at f632c71, past c7409f0: `install . --dry-run` still prints the question, defaults No, manages the session-start hook alone; `--yes --dry-run` places the 3 notes — the yes-path already converges, so the gap is a settled fork re-asked then answered wrong unattended. `install .temper --dry-run` would place a hook at `.temper/.claude/settings.json`. Blocker CHECK-WORKSPACE-DIR-RESOLVE shipped (c7409f0); gate opened. scoped at f632c71.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead — release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested at f632c71: no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, release.yml still defers darwin. scoped at b676656.
